meta.title: pyr

source.publication: Section 4: Roots, Prefixes, Suffixes and Foreign Words (owner-supplied reference)
source.usage_note: Personal study only. Root, words and meanings transcribed faithfully from the source PDF; a small number of well-attested real words on the same root are included where the source list was too short to form a complete family (4 to 8 members). Parts, glosses, context sentences, mentor note, attempt and construct options are the teaching layer, authored for the Language Garden (LANGUAGE_GARDEN_BIBLE §10).

meta.reviewer_notes: Batch 2: transcribed from the owner's roots (Greek and Latin) reference; 2 taught, 2 reach.

root.label: pyr
root.mentor_note: pyr is one of the few roots you can still feel burning inside the words it built.

attempt.direction_prompt: Drawn to quiet routine, or drawn to setting fires?

context_sentences: Investigators eventually traced the string of small warehouse fires to a single pyromaniac.
The film painted its villain as a pyromaniac long before he ever lit a match on screen.
By dusk the villagers had built the pyre high enough to be seen from the far shore.
The ceremony ended as it always had, with the pyre lit at the exact moment the sun set.
The stadium’s closing pyrotechnics lasted almost as long as the concert itself.
Years of pyrotechnics training went into a display that lasted barely ninety seconds.

construct_options.text: The skilled craft of making or setting off fireworks
The technical study of how fire spreads through a building
The engineering of heating systems for large buildings

context_sentences: The reaction was mildly pyrogenic, warming the flask enough that she reached for a glove.
A pyrogenic substance in the vaccine was later identified as the cause of the brief fevers.

construct_options.text: Producing heat or causing a fever
Resistant to catching fire under any condition
Producing a cooling or numbing sensation
